thinks.think: A static title card features white text on a gray gradient background. The upper text reads "Using & Measuring with an Outside Micrometer: Part 3". Centered in large font is "How to Read a Metric Micrometer". The bottom right corner displays a red "Starrett" logo with the black tagline "PRECISION, QUALITY, INNOVATION" beneath it.

The title card persists until the final moment, transitioning to a close-up of a silver micrometer's measurement scales. The stationary sleeve shows millimeter increments labeled 0 through 25, with half-millimeter marks below. The rotating thimble displays numbers 45, 0, and 5, alongside vertical text reading "NO. 230M" and a brand logo.

The video cuts from a static close-up of the measurement scales to a shot of hands holding the tool. A silver micrometer is positioned against a brass-colored cylindrical fitting. Fingers grip the frame and thimble. The background is a plain, light surface, and the Starrett logo remains visible in the corner.

The scene transitions to a new measurement setup. The brass cylindrical fitting is replaced by a flat, gray metal component with a stepped edge. Hands hold the silver micrometer, adjusting the thimble to measure the thickness of this new metallic object against the anvil and spindle.

A yellow bottle with a white cap dispenses clear liquid onto the tip of a silver metal rod held by a hand. The nozzle is positioned directly over the rod's end. The background is a plain, light surface. The red Starrett logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

The video cuts from the oil bottle to a static close-up of a silver micrometer. The sleeve shows upper markings 0 and 5, with lower half-millimeter lines. The thimble displays numbers 0 and 5, with vertical text "NO. 230M" visible. A hand grips the knurled end on the right, and the Starrett logo sits in the bottom right corner.

A close-up view features a silver micrometer held horizontally. The knurled thimble rotates clockwise, shifting the visible numbers from 5 to 10, then revealing 15 and 20. As the thimble turns, additional horizontal measurement lines on the stationary sleeve are exposed to the left. A hand grips the far right end.

A silver micrometer is held horizontally in a close-up view. The knurled thimble rotates, advancing the scale so that the numbers 30 and 35 become visible, replacing the previous 20 and 25 markings. The stationary sleeve lines stay exposed on the left, and a hand maintains its grip on the textured end at the right.

The silver micrometer's knurled thimble rotates further right. Scale markings shift to reveal numbers 40 and 45, then 0 aligns near the top edge. Vertical text "NO. 230M" becomes visible on the thimble's right side, while the sleeve's horizontal measurement lines remain stationary on the left.

A static graphic of the micrometer scales replaces the live view. White text overlaid at the top reads "graduations above the line = 1 millimeter." The sleeve scale shows numbers 0, 5, 10, and 15, while the thimble scale on the right displays 0, 45, 40, and 35.

The static micrometer scale graphic remains visible. A white vertical double-headed arrow labeled "1mm" appears above the sleeve scale, indicating the distance between adjacent upper graduation lines. A pink rectangular highlight simultaneously overlays the specific graduation mark immediately to the right of the number 5.

The overlay text changes to "graduations below the line = half millimeters." The pink highlight moves from an upper mark to a lower graduation beneath the horizontal line. A new label reading ".5mm" with arrows appears at the bottom, replacing the previous "1mm" indicator above.

The static graphic of the micrometer scales remains on screen. The text "graduations below the line = half millimeters" is displayed at the top. A pink highlight continues to mark a lower graduation beneath the horizontal line, accompanied by the ".5mm" label and arrows at the bottom.

The visual shifts to a silver micrometer held horizontally. A white text overlay at the top reads "beveled edge of thimble has 50 graduations." The knurled thimble rotates slightly, revealing scale numbers such as 35 on the beveled edge, while the sleeve's measurement lines remain stationary on the left.

The view zooms in on the micrometer scales with the text "each graduation = .01mm" overlaid at the top. A pink rectangular highlight appears on a specific graduation line on the thimble scale near the number 40, accompanied by a ".01mm" label to its right indicating the measurement value.

The on-screen text changes to "every fifth line is numbered." A pink highlight now covers the group of graduation lines on the thimble between the numbers 35 and 40. Additionally, two pink boxes displaying the numbers "40" and "35" appear next to their respective lines on the scale.

The scene cuts to a close-up of the micrometer sleeve and thimble edge without text overlays. The number 5 on the upper sleeve scale is highlighted in pink. The thimble scale displays numbers 25, 30, and 35 vertically. The horizontal reference line on the sleeve aligns with a graduation on the thimble between 25 and 30.

The static close-up continues, showing the micrometer scales. The number 5 on the upper sleeve is highlighted in pink. The thimble displays numbers 25, 30, and 35 vertically. The horizontal reference line aligns with a graduation on the thimble. The Starrett logo appears in the bottom right corner.

The close-up of the micrometer scales continues with the number 5 on the upper sleeve highlighted in pink. A horizontal pink bar appears, extending from the sleeve's reference line to the right. It highlights the specific graduation on the thimble scale aligning with the reference line, located between the numbers 25 and 30.

White text overlays the top reading "the '5' = 5.00mm". The horizontal pink highlight bar previously marking the thimble alignment disappears. The vertical pink highlight remains on the number 5 on the upper sleeve scale, indicating the measurement value on the silver instrument.

The static close-up persists with the text "the '5' = 5.00mm" displayed at the top. A pink highlight remains on the number 5 on the upper sleeve scale. The micrometer's thimble and sleeve scales, showing numbers 35, 30, and 25, stay in the same position with the Starrett logo visible in the bottom right corner.

The text overlay changes to "one additional 0.5mm line = 0.50mm". Simultaneously, the pink highlight moves from the upper '5' graduation to a lower graduation mark on the sleeve, located just past the '5' and beneath the horizontal reference line, indicating a half-millimeter increment. The thimble scale showing 35, 30, and 25 remains static.

The text overlay changes to "line '28' on thimble matches reading line on sleeve 28 x 0.01mm = 0.28mm". A horizontal pink bar appears, highlighting the alignment between the sleeve's reference line and a graduation on the thimble, replacing the previous vertical highlight on the sleeve scale.

The text overlay "line '28' on thimble matches reading line on sleeve 28 x 0.01mm = 0.28mm" stays visible at the top. A horizontal pink bar highlights the specific graduation on the thimble aligning with the sleeve's reference line, located between the 25 and 30 marks on the silver micrometer.

The text overlay changes to "for a measurement of 5.5" at the top. The horizontal pink highlight bar indicating the thimble alignment disappears. The close-up shot of the silver micrometer scales persists, showing numbers 25, 30, and 35 on the rotating thimble and 0 to 5 on the stationary sleeve.

The white text overlay at the top changes to read "for a measurement of 5.5 + .28". The static close-up of the silver micrometer persists, displaying the sleeve scale with the number 5 and the thimble scale with vertical numbers 25, 30, and 35 on the right.

The white text overlay at the top updates to display the final calculation result, reading "for a measurement of 5.5 + .28 = 5.78mm". The close-up shot of the silver micrometer remains static, showing the sleeve scale with the number 5 and the thimble scale with vertical numbers 25, 30, and 35.

The text overlay changes to "measures to 0.002mm". The view shifts to show a vernier scale on the sleeve with vertical numbers 0, 2, 4, 6, 8. The thimble displays numbers 35, 40, 45. The Starrett logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

A pink horizontal highlight appears over the number '4' on the vernier scale located on the sleeve. The text 'measures to 0.002mm' remains visible at the top. The thimble displays numbers 35, 40, and 45, while the Starrett logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

The white text overlay at the top updates to display "for a measurement of 5.784mm". Simultaneously, the pink horizontal highlight bar previously marking the number '4' on the vernier scale disappears. The silver micrometer scales, showing numbers 35, 40, and 45 on the thimble, remain visible without annotation.

The text overlay vanishes, leaving the micrometer scales visible briefly. Then, a white graphic appears titled "Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales" with "0.002mm Resolution". A pink rectangle highlights the vertical vernier scale numbers zero to eight on the left side of the diagram.

The static white diagram titled "Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales" remains on screen with "0.002mm Resolution" text. A pink rectangular highlight continues to cover the vertical vernier scale numbers zero through eight on the left side of the graphic. The "Starrett" logo is visible in the bottom right corner.

The white diagram titled "Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales" remains on screen with the pink highlight over the vernier numbers. New black text appears to the right of the thimble illustration, reading "• measures to 0.002mm".

The subtitle text updates to "0.001mm Resolution". The vernier scale lines within the pink highlighted box become significantly denser and more closely spaced. The black text bullet point on the right side vanishes, leaving only the diagram and title on the white background. The Starrett logo remains in the bottom right corner.

The diagram titled Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales shows 0.001mm Resolution. The pink box highlights dense vernier lines. New black text bullet point reading measures to 0.001mm appears to the right of the thimble scale. The Starrett logo remains visible in the bottom right corner of the white background.

The static diagram titled Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales with 0.001mm Resolution persists. A pink highlighted box covers the dense vernier scale lines on the left. To the right of the thimble illustration, the black text bullet point measures to 0.001mm remains visible alongside the Starrett logo in the bottom right corner.

A technical drawing appears on a white background featuring a red cylindrical component with flanged ends. Black text above reads "15.25mm ± .05mm" with arrows pointing to the red section's diameter. The Starrett logo sits in the bottom right corner.

The video transitions from a static diagram to a live-action close-up showing a person's hands holding a silver micrometer. The instrument measures a small, translucent white plastic component held between the anvil and spindle. The Starrett logo remains in the bottom right corner.

summary.text: [0-2] A static title card features white text "Using & Measuring with an Outside Micrometer: Part 3" and "How to Read a Metric Micrometer" on a gray gradient, with a red "Starrett" logo. [2-4] A close-up reveals silver micrometer scales: sleeve 0-25, thimble 45, 0, 5, and vertical text "NO. 230M". [4-8] Hands hold the silver micrometer against a brass cylindrical fitting, then a flat gray metal component. [8-10] A yellow bottle dispenses clear liquid onto a silver metal rod tip. [10-14] The micrometer is shown in close-up; the knurled thimble rotates from 5 to 20, exposing sleeve lines, with "NO. 230M" visible.

[14-18] A silver micrometer is held horizontally in a close-up. The knurled thimble rotates right, advancing the scale from 20/25 to 30/35, then revealing 40/45 and 0. Vertical text "NO. 230M" appears on the thimble. A hand grips the textured end.
[18-22] A static graphic replaces the view with text "graduations above the line = 1 millimeter". The sleeve shows 0, 5, 10, 15; the thimble shows 0, 45, 40, 35. A "1mm" label and white arrow appear above a pink highlight on an upper graduation right of 5.
[22-26] Text updates to "graduations below the line = half millimeters". The pink highlight shifts to a lower graduation beneath the line, accompanied by a ".5mm" label. The graphic remains.
[26-28] The view returns to the silver micrometer. Text "beveled edge of thimble has 50 graduations" is overlaid. The thimble rotates slightly, showing numbers like 35 on the beveled edge.

[34-36] Static close-up continues showing micrometer scales. Number 5 on upper sleeve highlighted pink. Thimble displays numbers 25, 30, 35 vertically. Horizontal reference line aligns with graduation on thimble. Starrett logo appears bottom right corner of frame.
[36-38] Close-up continues with number 5 on upper sleeve highlighted pink. Horizontal pink bar appears, extending from sleeve's reference line to the right. It highlights specific graduation on thimble scale aligning with reference line, located between numbers 25 and 30.
[38-40] White text overlays top reading "the '5' = 5.00mm". Horizontal pink highlight bar previously marking thimble alignment disappears. Vertical pink highlight remains on number 5 on upper sleeve scale, indicating measurement value on the silver instrument.
[40-42] Static close-up persists with text "the '5' = 5.00mm" displayed top. Pink highlight remains on number 5 on upper sleeve scale. Micrometer's thimble and sleeve scales, showing numbers 35, 30, 25, stay same position with Starrett logo visible bottom right corner.

[30-32] The on-screen text displays "every fifth line is numbered." A pink highlight covers the group of graduation lines on the thimble between the numbers 35 and 40. Additionally, two pink boxes displaying the numbers "40" and "35" appear next to their respective lines on the scale. [32-34] The scene cuts to a close-up of the micrometer sleeve and thimble edge. The number 5 on the upper sleeve scale is highlighted in pink. The thimble scale displays numbers 25, 30, and 35 vertically. [42-44] The text overlay changes to "one additional 0.5mm line = 0.50mm". Simultaneously, the pink highlight moves from the upper '5' graduation to a lower graduation mark on the sleeve, located just past the '5' and beneath the horizontal reference line. [44-46] The text overlay changes to "line '28' on thimble matches reading line on sleeve 28 x 0.01mm = 0.28mm". A horizontal pink bar appears, highlighting the alignment between the sleeve's reference line and a graduation on the thimble.

[48-50] The text overlay reads "for a measurement of 5.5" as the pink highlight bar disappears from the silver micrometer showing sleeve numbers 0-5 and thimble numbers 25, 30, 35. [50-52] The overlay updates to "for a measurement of 5.5 + .28" while the static micrometer view persists. [52-54] The text completes the equation: "for a measurement of 5.5 + .28 = 5.78mm" over the static shot. [54-56] The overlay changes to "measures to 0.002mm" as the view shifts to a vernier scale on the sleeve (vertical numbers 0, 2, 4, 6, 8) and thimble (35, 40, 45), with the Starrett logo visible.

[58-60] White text "for a measurement of 5.784mm" displays as the pink highlight on vernier '4' disappears, revealing silver micrometer scales (35, 40, 45). [60-62] The overlay vanishes; a white graphic titled "Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales" with "0.002mm Resolution" appears, showing a pink rectangle highlighting vertical vernier numbers 0-8. [62-64] The static diagram remains with the pink highlight over numbers 0-8 and the "Starrett" logo in the bottom right corner. [64-66] New black text "• measures to 0.002mm" appears to the right of the thimble illustration on the diagram.

[66-68] The subtitle updates to "0.001mm Resolution". Vernier scale lines within the pink highlighted box become significantly denser. The black text bullet point vanishes. [68-70] A black text bullet point reading "measures to 0.001mm" appears to the right of the thimble scale. The Starrett logo is visible. [70-72] The static diagram titled "Metric Micrometers Vernier Scales" persists with the dense vernier lines and text. [72-74] A technical drawing appears featuring a red cylindrical component with flanged ends. Black text above reads "15.25mm ± .05mm" with arrows pointing to the red section's diameter. The Starrett logo sits in the bottom right.

[74-76] The video transitions to a live-action close-up showing hands holding a silver micrometer. The instrument measures a small, translucent white plastic component held between the anvil and spindle. The Starrett logo remains in the bottom right corner. [76-78] The view cuts to a close-up of the silver micrometer scales, showing sleeve markings 10 and 15 and thimble markings 20 and 25. White text "15.25mm" appears at the top center. Horizontal graduation lines run along the sleeve while vertical lines mark the thimble, with the Starrett logo visible in the bottom right. [78-80] The view displays silver micrometer scales with sleeve numbers 10, 15 and thimble numbers 20, 25. White text "15.25mm" sits at the top. Below it, smaller white text "± .05mm" appears. A distinct black checkmark symbol fades in to the right of the measurement text. [80-82] The scene transitions to a new micrometer close-up set against a plain gray background. The sleeve displays 0 and 5, while the thimble shows 5, 0, 45, and 40. The previous measurement text and checkmark are gone. The Starrett logo persists in the bottom right corner.

From 82s to 88s, a static close-up features a silver micrometer scale against a gray gradient background. The sleeve displays upper numbers "0" and "5" with lower graduation marks, while the thimble shows vertical numbers "5", "0", "45", and "40". The "Starrett" logo remains fixed in the bottom right corner. At 88s, a second micrometer scale graphic appears in the lower right area, displaying sleeve numbers "25" and "30" and a thimble with visible digits "5", "0", and "4". The original scale remains positioned in the upper left.

[90-94] Two micrometer scale graphics appear against a gray background; the upper graphic remains static while the lower graphic, showing sleeve numbers 25 and 30, moves right. A new graphic appears in the center foreground displaying sleeve numbers 50 and 55. The Starrett logo is visible.
[94-96] Three micrometer scale illustrations arrange against a gray gradient. The upper left graphic displays sleeve numbers 0 and 5. The upper right shows 25 and 30. The lower left presents sleeve markings 50 and 55 with thimble graduations 40 to 5.
[96-98] A fourth graphic appears in the lower right, displaying sleeve numbers 75 and 80 with thimble markings 5, 0, 45, and 40. The previous three illustrations remain stationary. The Starrett logo is visible.

From 98s to 106s, the video shows four silver micrometer scale illustrations arranged in a two-by-two grid against a gray gradient background. The top left scale reads 0-5, top right 25-30, bottom left 50-55, and bottom right 75-80. Each graphic features detailed sleeve and thimble graduations with numerical markings. The 'Starrett' logo is visible in the bottom right corner. The layout remains static throughout the duration. The view is fixed on the illustrations, showing no camera movement or transitions. The white surfaces of the scales contrast with the gray background. The measurements are clearly legible.

[106-114] The video maintains a static shot of four silver micrometer scale illustrations arranged in a two-by-two grid against a gray gradient background. The top row features scales starting at 0 and 25, marked 0-5 and 25-30. The bottom row shows scales beginning at 50 and 75, marked 50-55 and 75-80. Each graphic features detailed sleeve and thimble graduations with numerical markings. The Starrett logo remains visible in the bottom right corner. No visual changes occur during this interval, as the static view persists with the same four scale illustrations and logo placement throughout the duration.
